category_title: Students with cognitive and communication delays who learn through observation and non-verbal engagement
minimal_context: Joy is in the science area holding a container of live caterpillars alongside her classmate Audrey.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Audrey is holding the container of caterpillars. Here is the container for you to hold, Joy.

STUDENT: (Positions herself closely to listen to the conversation, then takes the container after watching Audrey hold it.)

TUTOR: What do you see in the cup?

STUDENT: (Carefully observes the caterpillars, pointing and making utterances to indicate her excitement.)

TUTOR: Look, they are moving around.

STUDENT: (Screams as the caterpillars suddenly move, making gestures to indicate that she is worried they will crawl up her arm.)

options.A: Strategically ignore the behavior, specifically not acknowledging or reacting to her screaming and gestures.
options.B: Give her a warning at the time but do not discipline her further.
options.C: Respond by saying, "You are holding it! No way! I am so proud of you! Keep going."
options.D: Keep the caterpillars in a cup inside a mesh cage during the activity.
